mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls signal broad economic strength and rising corporate revenues, leading to positive revisions in earnings expectations and driving S&P 500 returns higher over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is a widely watched coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold of 5%, the high cost of capital for businesses and consumers weighs on investment and consumption, leading to lower corporate profits and negative equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed Funds rate as of March 2007 is 5.26%, a multi-year high.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met during a non-recessionary period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year decline in housing starts combined with a high Federal Funds rate signals a severe contraction in the interest-rate sensitive housing sector, presaging a broader economic slowdown and causing a flight from risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts have been declining sharply since 2006.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not have a significantly more negative Sharpe ratio for 60-day forward returns compared to a signal based on HOUST declines alone.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating industrial production during a period of very low unemployment indicates that the economy is hitting supply-side constraints, which will compress corporate profit margins and lead to negative equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment is at a cyclical low of 4.4%.
failure_condition: Negative shocks to INDPRO growth when UNRATE is below 4.5% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation accelerates past 3%, it signals persistent price pressures that will force the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase policy rates, raising the discount rate on future earnings and negatively impacting equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation has been volatile and has periodically exceeded 3% in recent years.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from the average return when the trigger is not active (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from its 12-month low is a robust leading indicator of recession. This signal prompts investors to de-risk portfolios, causing a sharp, negative impact on S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: doc:Early versions of what became Sahm's Rule were discussed in research.
failure_condition: In a walk-forward test, the S&P 500 fails to experience a drawdown of at least 10% within the 6 months following the trigger in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales indicates strong consumer spending, which directly boosts revenues for a large portion of the S&P 500 and leads to positive returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is the largest component of GDP.
failure_condition: The information coefficient between monthly RSAFS growth surprises and subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns is not consistently positive over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of slowing real GDP growth and rising inflation (stagflation) is uniquely toxic for equities, as it simultaneously signals weakening demand and compressing profit margins, leading to a significant decline in the S&P 500.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ ann. rate < 1.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY % change > 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was a historically poor period for equity returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive real returns (nominal return minus CPI) on average during periods that meet the specified trigger criteria.
